overview.tagline: A diverse, arts-friendly borough with a creative spirit just minutes from the George Washington Bridge
overview.description: Leonia is a compact, culturally diverse borough in southeastern Bergen County known for its artistic community, excellent schools, and exceptional proximity to New York City via the George Washington Bridge. The borough has a long history as a haven for artists, writers, and musicians, and today that creative spirit continues through community theater, art programs, and a welcoming, eclectic atmosphere. With about 9,000 residents packed into just over one square mile, Leonia offers walkable neighborhoods, a tight-knit community, and one of the shortest commutes to Manhattan in Bergen County.
highlights: Minutes from the George Washington Bridge — one of the fastest NYC commutes in Bergen County
Historic artists' colony with a vibrant cultural community
Diverse, welcoming population with strong international character
Walkable borough with parks, shops, and restaurants along Broad Avenue

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Leonia's compact size and proximity to NYC keep property values strong.
realEstate.recentTrends: Leonia's real estate market benefits from its proximity to the George Washington Bridge and its diverse, walkable character. Demand is strong for single-family homes and the borough's limited inventory keeps prices competitive. Condominiums and townhomes also see steady interest from commuters.
neighborhoodNotes: Broad Avenue area — Walking distance to shops, restaurants, and the borough's commercial center
Grand Avenue — Quiet residential street with well-maintained homes
Fort Lee Road corridor — Mix of residential and some multi-family properties
Leonia Heights — Slightly elevated area with larger homes and scenic views

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Leonia.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through the DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same schedule
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Yard waste collected seasonally
Electronics and hazardous waste collection events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water and sewer service is managed by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water New Jersey
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Quarterly water billing through Veolia
Water quality reports published annually
Emergency water service available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages on-street parking and municipal lots throughout the borough.
details: Metered parking along Broad Avenue in the commercial district
Municipal parking lots available near downtown
Commuter parking at the NJ Transit bus stop areas
Overnight parking restrictions on borough streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced

additionalServices.title: Leonia Public Library
additionalServices.description: Well-regarded public library offering programs, books, digital resources, and community space.
details: Located at 227 Fort Lee Road
Children's story times, teen programs, and adult book clubs
Diverse collection reflecting the community's multicultural character
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for all ages.
details: Youth sports, arts programs, and summer camps
Adult fitness and cultural programs
Senior citizen activities and trips
Community events and performances

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Leonia enforces noise regulations to maintain quality of life in its dense residential neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Amplified music and loud gatherings subject to enforcement after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must comply with borough height and setback requirements
Properties must be kept free of debris and unsightly conditions
Abandoned vehicles must be removed

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Leonia requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Nuisance barking subject to enforcement
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is tightly regulated in Leonia due to the borough's density and proximity to the GWB.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets without a permit
Cut-through traffic restrictions enforced to deter GWB-bound commuters
Metered parking along Broad Avenue with time limits
Snow emergency parking bans — vehicles must be off-street
Handicapped parking violations subject to significant fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough housing codes and registration requirements.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to code standards
Inspections conducted at change of tenancy
Multi-family dwellings subject to additional requirements

other.title: Traffic Cut-Through Restrictions
other.summary: Leonia has enacted restrictions on cut-through traffic to protect residential streets from GWB-bound commuters using navigation apps.
keyRules: Certain borough streets restricted to residents only during peak hours
Non-resident drivers subject to fines for violating cut-through restrictions
Signage posted at key entry points identifying restricted streets
Restrictions typically in effect during morning and evening rush hours

publicSchools.name: Leonia High School
publicSchools.notes: Diverse high school with strong AP offerings, arts programs, and competitive athletics

publicSchools.name: Leonia Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school with a comprehensive curriculum and active extracurriculars

publicSchools.name: Anna C. Scott Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the borough with a diverse student body and strong community programs

nearbyHigherEd: New Jersey City University (Jersey City) — 25 minutes

whyMoveHere: One of the shortest commutes to Manhattan in Bergen County via the George Washington Bridge
Culturally diverse community with an artistic, welcoming atmosphere
Well-regarded public schools with diverse student populations
Walkable borough with shops and restaurants along Broad Avenue
More affordable than neighboring Fort Lee and Englewood Cliffs
Strong sense of community with active civic organizations

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus / Car via George Washington Bridge
commuteToNYC.duration: 15-30 minutes to Midtown Manhattan by bus; 10-20 minutes by car to the GWB
commuteToNYC.notes: Leonia's proximity to the George Washington Bridge makes it one of the fastest Bergen County commutes to Manhattan. NJ Transit buses run frequently to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Many residents also drive to the bridge or use the nearby Palisades Interstate Parkway.

costOfLiving.summary: Leonia offers a relatively moderate cost of living for its proximity to NYC, with housing prices below Fort Lee and Englewood Cliffs but above the Bergen County median.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 20% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Exceptional proximity to NYC via the George Washington Bridge
Diverse, inclusive community with a creative, artistic character
Good public schools with small-town feel
Walkable downtown with local shops and restaurants
Lower home prices than neighboring Fort Lee and Englewood Cliffs
cons: GWB-related traffic can impact local streets during rush hours
Compact borough with smaller lot sizes
Property taxes are high relative to home values
No train station — reliant on bus service for transit
Limited parking in the commercial district

restaurants.name: Leonia Classic Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with traditional American fare and generous portions.

restaurants.name: Origin Thai
restaurants.description: Authentic Thai restaurant offering curries, noodle dishes, and traditional Thai specialties in a casual setting.

restaurants.name: Saigon Kitchen
restaurants.description: Vietnamese restaurant serving pho, banh mi, and other Vietnamese classics. Popular with locals for authentic flavors.

restaurants.name: The Pizza Place
restaurants.description: Popular neighborhood pizzeria offering New York-style pizza, calzones, and Italian favorites.

parks.name: Overpeck County Park
parks.description: Large Bergen County park bordering Leonia, featuring walking and biking trails, sports fields, a driving range, equestrian trails, and beautiful views of the Overpeck Creek.

parks.name: Wood Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with playgrounds, sports fields, and open green space. A popular gathering spot for families.

parks.name: Sylvan Street Park
parks.description: Small community park with a playground and sitting area, convenient for local families.

events.name: Leonia Arts Festival
events.description: Annual arts festival celebrating the borough's creative heritage with art exhibits, performances, live music, and family activities.

events.name: Leonia Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair on Broad Avenue with vendors, food, live entertainment, and community activities.
events.when: Fall, typically in September or October

events.name: Holiday Celebration and Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting, music, and community gathering at Borough Hall.

attractions.name: Overpeck County Park
attractions.description: Expansive county park with trails, sports facilities, and natural areas along Overpeck Creek. A major recreational resource for Leonia residents and visitors.

attractions.name: Leonia Players
attractions.description: Community theater group performing plays and musicals, reflecting the borough's long artistic tradition.

attractions.name: Fort Lee Historic Park
attractions.description: Revolutionary War site in neighboring Fort Lee with panoramic views of the George Washington Bridge and the Hudson River.

shoppingAreas: Broad Avenue — Leonia's main commercial street with local shops and restaurants
Fort Lee — nearby dining and shopping along Main Street and Lemoine Avenue
Edgewater — The Shops at Mitsuwa and River Road retail

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 1st and 3rd Monday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 312 Broad Avenue, Leonia, NJ 07605

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
